strings.chat.context_full_placeholder: This chat context is full. Create a new chat or open another one.
strings.chat.intro: Describe what you want to change in the sound. I will save the answer as a new preset and apply it.
strings.chat.placeholder: For example: reduce boom, add air, vocals are harsh
strings.dialog.new_reserved: The name New is reserved for a new preset.
strings.dialog.save_changes_text: Do you want to save these changes?
strings.filter_type_desc.band_pass: Keeps a band around the selected frequency and attenuates everything outside it. Useful for narrow, telephone-like effects or focusing on one range.
strings.filter_type_desc.high_pass: Lets frequencies above the cutoff pass and reduces lows below it. Commonly used to remove rumble, handling noise, or excessive sub-bass.
strings.filter_type_desc.high_shelf: Raises or lowers the high-frequency range above the selected frequency. Useful for adding air or softening brightness.
strings.filter_type_desc.low_pass: Lets frequencies below the cutoff pass and reduces highs above it. Useful for darkening sound or removing hiss and harsh top end.
strings.filter_type_desc.low_shelf: Raises or lowers the low-frequency range below the selected frequency. Useful for bass weight or reducing boom.
strings.filter_type_desc.notch: Cuts a narrow area around the selected frequency. Useful for removing hum, whistle, ringing, or a specific resonance.
strings.filter_type_desc.peaking: Raises or lowers a bell-shaped area around the selected frequency. Useful for precise tonal correction and general EQ moves.
strings.filter_param_desc.freq: Sets the center or cutoff frequency in hertz. This is the main point of the spectrum the filter is built around.
strings.filter_param_desc.gain: Sets how much the filter raises or lowers the sound in dB. On pass and notch filters it works as overall makeup gain for the filter shape.
strings.filter_param_desc.q: Controls the filter width. Higher Q is narrower and more surgical; lower Q is wider and smoother.
strings.filter_param_desc.type: Chooses the filter shape: bell, shelves, pass filters, band pass, or notch. The shape defines how frequency, gain, and Q affect the curve.
strings.settings.allow_cpu: Allow CPU usage for AI responses
strings.settings.title: Settings
strings.toast.ai_still_answering: The AI agent is still answering
strings.toast.current_preset_unsaved: The current preset is not saved yet
strings.toast.wait_ai: Wait for the AI agent response
